title: Ourouparia sclerophylla
intro: Ourouparia sclerophylla, also known as Ourouparia, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family native to Madagascar. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Ourouparia sclerophylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Ourouparia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Ourouparia sclerophylla belongs to the family Gomortegaceae. This plant is native to tropical regions and is a part of the Laurales order. The classification of Ourouparia sclerophylla is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - angiosperms, clade - eudicots, clade - asterids, order - Laurales, family - Gomortegaceae, genus - Ourouparia, species - O. sclerophylla. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but Gomortegaceae is widely accepted as its family. It is a rare and lesser-known species within its family.

The plant's extracts are believed to exhibit antioxidant and cytoprotective activities, which may help in protecting cells from damage. In some studies, Ourouparia sclerophylla has shown promise in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders.
